corners.name: source-brace-is-a-literal-value
corners.why: IL strips the braces and keeps the body RAW, never lowering it to (evl …). topic-174102/post-0009-snippet-01 @ 7.22.1

corners.name: source-literal-containing-brace-and-semicolon
corners.why: a literal body may itself contain scope-looking text; descending into it is the audit's #8/H5 bug
corners.verified: CHR 7.23.2: IL keeps the whole source={…} (nested braces + inner `;`) RAW, braces stripped, never lowered to (evl …).

corners.name: script-brace-is-a-literal-value
corners.why: `script` never appears as a lowered block anywhere in the corpus

corners.name: do-else-are-sibling-scopes
corners.why: both lower to blocks; serialized in name order per N4

corners.name: on-error-is-a-scope
corners.why: `:do {` body lowers under the IL name `command`; sorted before `on-error` by N4

corners.name: in-brace-after-foreach-is-an-array-literal
corners.why: lowers to the LIST `in=p12;pem`, not a block. eworm/check-certificates.rsc @ 7.22.1

corners.name: array-literal-value-is-not-a-scope
corners.why: an array literal is a value; Q1's container rule already refuses to expand it

corners.name: array-literal-with-named-keys-is-not-a-scope
corners.why: `black=`/`red=` are dictionary keys, observed only as literals. topic-168093/post-0001-snippet-02

corners.name: scope-inside-a-bracket-substitution-is-not-a-top-level-block
corners.why: `[…]` is Q3's question, not a block

corners.name: literal-then-scope-in-one-statement
corners.why: a literal does not become a scope by sitting next to other arguments
corners.verified: CHR 7.23.2: IL keeps source literal even beside comment=y (args alphabetized, source body raw).
